obito_do_autor.original:
Algum tempo hesitei se devia abrir estas memorias pelo principio ou
pelo fim, isto é, se poria em primeiro logar o meu nascimento ou a
minha morte.
obito_do_autor.translation: For a while I hesitated whether I should open these memories at the beginning or the end, that is, whether I should first log my birth or my death.

obito_do_autor.original:
Supposto o uso vulgar seja começar pelo nascimento, duas
considerações me levaram a adoptar differente methodo: a primeira é que
eu não sou propriamente um autor defunto, mas um defunto autor, para
quem a campa foi outro berço; a segunda é que o escripto ficaria assim
mais galante e mais novo.
obito_do_autor.translation: Assuming the usual usage is to begin at birth, two considerations led me to adopt a different method: the first is that I am not actually a deceased author, but a deceased author, for whom the grave was another cradle; the second is that the scribe would thus become more gallant and younger.

o_delirio.original:
Os seculos desfilavam n'um turbilhão, e, não obstante,
porque os olhos do delirio são outros, eu via tudo o que passava diante
de mim,—flagellos e delicias,—desde essa cousa que se chama gloria
até essa outra que se chama miseria, e via o amor multiplicando a
miseria, e via a miseria aggravando a debilidade.
o_delirio.translation: The centuries were in a whirlwind, and yet, because the eyes of delusion are different, I saw everything that passed before me, flagellations and delights, from this thing called glory to this other thing called misery, and I saw love multiplying misery, and I saw misery aggravating weakness.

o_delirio.original:
Ahi vinham a cobiça
que devora, a colera que inflamma, a inveja que baba, e a enxada e a
penna, humidas de suor, e a ambição, a fome, a vaidade, a melancholia,
a riqueza, o amor, e todos agitavam o homem, como um chocalho, até
destruil-o, como um farrapo.
o_delirio.translation: Ah, greed that devours, anger that burns, envy that feeds, and fever and feathers, wet with sweat, and ambition, hunger, vanity, melancholy, wealth, love, and all stirred man up like a stork, until he destroyed him like a frog.

o_delirio.original:

digere-me.
o_delirio.translation: Come on, Pandora, open your belly, and digest me; the thing is funny, but digest me.

o_delirio.original:
A resposta foi compellir-me fortemente a olhar para baixo, e a ver os
seculos que continuavam a passar, velozes e turbulentos, as gerações
que se superpunham ás gerações, umas tristes, como os Hebreus do
captiveiro, outras alegres, como os devassos de Commodo, e todas
ellas pontuaes na sepultura.
o_delirio.translation: The answer was to force me strongly to look down, and to see the centuries that kept on passing, fast and turbulent, the generations that overlapped with the generations, some sad, like the captive Hebrews, some joyful, like the ravaged of Commodus, and all of them dotted in the grave.

o_delirio.original:
Quiz fugir, mas uma força mysteriosa me
retinha os pés; então disse commigo:—«Bem, os seculos vão passando,
chegará o meu, e passará tambem, até o ultimo, que me dará a decifração
da eternidade.» E fixei os olhos, e continuei a ver as edades, que
vinham chegando e passando, já então tranquillo e resoluto, não sei
até se alegre.
o_delirio.translation: I might run away, but a mysterious force held my feet; and I said, "Well, the ages pass, mine will come, and it will pass also, to the last, which will give me the decipherment of eternity". And I closed my eyes, and kept seeing the ages, which came and went, now calm and determined, I do not know whether to rejoice.

o_delirio.original: Talvez alegre.
o_delirio.translation: Maybe he's happy.

o_delirio.original:
Cada seculo trazia a sua porção de
sombra e de luz, de apathia e de combate, de verdade e de erro, e o
seu cortejo de systemas, de idéas novas, de novas illusões; em cada
um delles rebentavam as verduras de uma primavera, e amarelleciam
depois, para remoçar mais tarde.
o_delirio.translation: Each century brought its share of shadow and light, of apathy and combat, of truth and error, and its cortex of systems, of new ideas, of new illusions; in each one of them the vegetables of a spring were painted, and then yellowed, to be removed later.

Ao passo que a vida tinha assim uma
regularidade de calendario, fazia-se a historia e a civilisação, e o
homem, nu e desarmado, armava-se e vestia-se, construia o tugurio e
o palacio, a rude aldêa e Thebas de cem portas, creava a sciencia,
que perscruta, e a arte que enleva, fazia-se orador, mecanico,
philosopho, corria a face do globo, descia ao ventre da terra, subia
á esphera das nuvens, collaborando assim na obra mysteriosa, com que
entretinha a necessidade da vida e a melancholia do desamparo.
o_delirio.translation: While life had thus a regularity of calendar, history and civilization were made, and man, naked and unarmed, armed and dressed, built the tugurium and palace, the rude alley and the Thebes of a hundred doors, created science, which permeated, and the art that it takes, became a speaker, a mechanic, a philosopher, ran across the globe, descended into the belly of the earth, climbed to the sphere of clouds, thus collaborating in the mysterious work, with which he entertained the necessity of life and the melancholy of helplessness.

o_menino_pai_do_homen.translation: Yes, my father adored me, he had this love without merit for me, which is a simple and strong impulse of the flesh; a love that reason neither contrasts nor rules.

o_menino_pai_do_homen.original:
Minha mãe era uma senhora fraca, de pouco cerebro e muito
coração, assaz credula, sinceramente piedosa,—caseira, apezar de
bonita, e modesta, apezar de abastada; temente ás trovoadas e ao

o_menino_pai_do_homen.translation: My mother was a weak lady, with little brain and much heart, a credulous ass, sincerely pious, a housewife, a handsome, and modest, a rich woman, afraid of thunder and her husband.

o_menino_pai_do_homen.original: O marido era na terra o seu deus.
o_menino_pai_do_homen.translation: Her husband was her god on earth.

o_menino_pai_do_homen.original:

o_menino_pai_do_homen.translation: From the collaboration of these two creatures, my education was born, which, if there was anything good, was generally vicious, incomplete, and in part negative.

o_menino_pai_do_homen.original:
Meu tio conego

mais liberdade do que ensino, e mais affeição do que emenda; mas meu
pae respondia que applicava na minha educação um systema inteiramente
superior ao systema usado; e por este modo, sem confundir o irmão,
illudia-se a si proprio.
o_menino_pai_do_homen.translation: My uncle Conego sometimes made some corrections to my brother; he told him that she gave me more freedom than teaching, and more affection than amending; but my father replied that he applied in my education a system altogether superior to the system used; and thus, without confusing the brother, he deluded himself.

a_borboleta_preta.translation: I remembered the evening's case, and laughed; I immediately thought of the daughter of Mr Eusebius, the fear she had, and the dignity she had retained.

a_borboleta_preta.translation: It was dark as night; and the gentle gesture with which, once set, he began to move his nails, had a certain mocking air, a kind of mephistophelic irony, which bore me very much.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Nariz, consciencia sem remorsos, tu me valeste muito na vida... Já
meditaste alguma vez no destino do nariz, amado leitor?
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: Nose, unrepentant conscience, you have been very valuable to me in life... have you ever meditated on the fate of the nose, dear reader?

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
A explicação
do doutor Pangloss é que o nariz foi creado para uso dos oculos,—e
tal explicação confesso que até certo tempo me pareceu definitiva; mas
veiu um dia, em que, estando a ruminar esse e outros pontos obscuros de
philosophia, atinei com a unica, verdadeira e definitiva explicação.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: Dr. Pangloss's explanation is that the nose was created for the use of the eyes, and such an explanation I confess that for a time it seemed definitive to me; but I saw one day, while ruminating on this and other obscure points of philosophy, I came upon the one, true and definitive explanation.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original: Com effeito, bastou-me attentar no costume do fakir.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: I was just trying to keep up with the fakir.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Sabe o leitor
que o fakir gasta longas horas a olhar para a ponta do nariz, com
o fim unico de ver a luz celeste.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: The reader knows that the fakir spends long hours looking at the tip of his nose, with the sole purpose of seeing the celestial light.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Quando elle finca os olhos na
ponta do nariz, perde o sentimento das cousas externas, embelleza-se
no invisivel, apprehende o impalpavel, desvincula-se da terra,
dissolve-se, etherisa-se.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: When she closes her eyes to the tip of her nose, she loses the sense of outer things, she embellishes herself in the invisible, she grasps the untouchable, she disconnects from the earth, she dissolves, she etherises.

a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: This sublimation of the being by the tip of the nose is the most excellent phenomenon of the spirit; and the faculty of obtaining it does not belong to fakir alone; it is universal.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Cada homem tem necessidade
e poder de contemplar o seu proprio nariz, para o fim de ver a luz
celeste; e tal contemplação, cujo effeito é a subordinação do universo
a um nariz sómente, constitue o equilibrio das sociedades.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: Every man has the need and power to contemplate his own nose in order to see the heavenly light; and such contemplation, the effect of which is the subordination of the universe to a single nose, constitutes the balance of societies.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Se os
narizes se contemplassem exclusivamente uns aos outros, o genero humano
não chegaria a durar dois séculos: extinguia-se com as primeiras tribos.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: If the noses were to look only at each other, the human race would not last two centuries: it would be extinct with the first tribes.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Ouço daqui uma objecção do leitor:—Como pode ser assim, diz elle,
se nunca jamais ninguem não viu estarem os homens a contemplar o seu
proprio nariz?
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: I hear an objection from the reader: 'How can it be so,' says she, 'when no one has ever seen men stare at their own noses?

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Leitor obtuso, isso prova que nunca entraste no cerebro de um
chapeleiro.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: Obsessed reader, that proves you never entered the brains of a hatter.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Um chapeleiro passa por uma loja de chapeus; é a loja de
um rival, que a abriu ha dois annos; tinha então duas portas, hoje tem
quatro; promette ter seis e oito.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: A hatter passes by a hat shop; it is the store of a rival, who opened it two years ago; it had two doors then, today it has four; it promises to have six and eight.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
Nas vidraças ostentam-se os chapeus
do rival; pelas portas entram os freguezes do rival; e o chapeleiro
compara aquella loja com a sua, que é mais antiga e tem só duas portas,
e aquelles chapeus com os seus, menos buscados, ainda que de egual
preço.
a_ponta_do_nariz.translation: The windows show the hat of the rival; the doors enter the hosts of the rival; and the hat maker compares that shop to his, which is older and has only two doors, and those hats to his, less sought after, though of equal price.

a_ponta_do_nariz.original:
A conclusão, portanto, é que ha duas forças capitaes: o amor, que

minha.translation: My conscience waved so much in the evening, that it became suffocated, without breathing; but the restitution of the half-fold was a window that opened to the other side of morality; a wave of pure air entered, and the poor lady breathed loose.

a_casinha.translation: The Baroness told her frankly that there had been much talk in the theatre the night before about my absence from the Snow Wolf cabin; they had commented on my relations in the house; in short, we were the object of public suspicion.

a_casinha.original:
A casa

dentro era o infinito, um mundo eterno, superior, excepcional, nosso,
somente nosso, sem leis, sem instituições, sem baronezas, sem olheiros,
sem escutas,—um só mundo, um só casal, uma só vida, uma só vontade,
uma só affeição,—a unidade moral de todas as cousas pela exclusão das
que me eram contrarias.
a_casinha.translation: The house saved me everything; the ordinary world would end at the door; I looked inward into the infinite, an eternal, superior, exceptional world, ours alone, without laws, without institutions, without barons, without eyes, without listeners, one world, one couple, one life, one will, one affection, the moral unity of all things by excluding those that were contrary to me.

o_seno_do_livro.translation: You are in a hurry to grow old, and the book is slow; you love the right and nourished narrative, the regular and flowing style, and this book and my style are like drunkards, twisting to the right and to the left, walking and stopping, whispering, screaming, laughing, threatening the sky, slipping and coming... And here they are!

o_humanitismo.original:

destinado a arruinar todos os demais systemas.
o_humanitismo.translation: As for the Fifth Dorn, I would like to end by saying Humanism, a philosophical system designed to ruin all other systems.

o_humanitismo.original:
—Humanitas, dizia elle, o principio das cousas, não é outro senão o
mesmo homem repartido por todos os homens.
o_humanitismo.translation: "Humanites", she said, "the beginning of things is but the same man divided among all men.

o_humanitismo.original:
Conta tres phases Humanitas;
astatica, anterior a toda a creação; aexpansiva, começo das
cousas; adispersiva, apparecimento do homem; e contará mais uma,
acontractiva, absorpção do homem e das cousas.
o_humanitismo.translation: It contains three phases Humanitas; astatica, prior to all creation; the expansive, the beginning of things; the dispersive, the appearance of man; and it will tell one more, contractive, the absorption of man and things.

o_humanitismo.original:
Aexpansão,
iniciando o universo, suggeriu a Humanitas o desejo de o gozar, e dahi
adispersão, que não é mais do que a multiplicação personificada da
substancia original.
o_humanitismo.translation: Expansion, beginning the universe, suggested to Humanitas the desire to enjoy it, and thus dispersion, which is nothing more than the personified multiplication of the original substance.

o_humanitismo.original:
Como me não apparecesse assaz clara esta exposição, o Quincas Borba
desenvolveu-a de um modo profundo, fazendo notar as grandes linhas
do systema.
o_humanitismo.translation: Since this exposition did not seem clear to me, the Quincas Borba developed it in a profound way, making the main lines of the system clear.

o_humanitismo.original:
Explicou-me que, por um lado, o Humanitismo ligava-se ao
Brahmanismo, a saber, na distribuição dos homens pelas differentes
partes do corpo de Humanitas; mas aquillo que na religião indiana
tinha apenas uma estreita significação theologica e politica, era no
Humanitismo a grande lei do valor pessoal.
o_humanitismo.translation: He explained to me that on the one hand Humanitism was connected with Brahmanism, namely, the distribution of men among the different parts of the human body; but while in Indian religion it had only a narrow theological and political significance, it was in Humanitism that the great law of personal value was established.

o_humanitismo.original:
Assim, descender do peito
ou dos rins de Humanitas, isto é, serum forte, não era o mesmo que
descender dos cabellos ou da ponta do nariz.
o_humanitismo.translation: So coming down from the chest or kidneys of Humanitas, that is, strong serum, was not the same as coming down from the hair or the tip of the nose.

o_humanitismo.original:
Dahi a necessidade de
cultivar e temperar o musculo.

o_humanitismo.original:
Hercules ou Herakles não foi senão um
symbolo antecipado do Humanitismo.
o_humanitismo.translation: Hercules or Herakles was but an early symbol of Humanism.

o_humanitismo.translation: Since life is the greatest benefit of the universe, and there is no beggar who does not prefer misery to death (which is a delightful influx of Humanitas), it follows that the transmission of life, far from being an occasion of gallantry, is the supreme hour of the spiritual mass.

o_humanitismo.original:
—Imagina, por exemplo, que eu não tinha nascido, continuou o Quincas

o_humanitismo.translation: Imagine, for example, that I had not been born, continued the Fifth Fool; it is positive that I would not now have the pleasure of talking to you, eating this potato, going to the theater, and to say it all in one word: live.

o_humanitismo.original:
Nota que eu não faço do homem um simples vehiculo de Humanitas;

proprio.
o_humanitismo.translation: Note that I do not make man a mere vehicle of Humanitas; no, she is at once a vehicle, a coachman and a passenger; she is my own reduced Humanitas; I have given the need to worship myself.

o_humanitismo.original: Queres uma prova da superioridade do meu systema?

o_humanitismo.original:

o_humanitismo.translation: There is no Greek or Turkish moralist, Christian or Muslim, who does not thunder against the feeling of envy.

o_humanitismo.original:

o_humanitismo.translation: The agreement is universal, from the fields of Idumea to the top of Tijuca.

o_humanitismo.translation: The last volume was a political treatise, based on Humanism; it was perhaps the most boring part of the system, since it was designed with a formidable rigor of logic.

o_humanitismo.original:
Reorganisada a

a insurreição, o simples murro, a facada anonyma, a miseria, a fome, as
doenças; mas sendo esses suppostos flagellos verdadeiros equivocos do
entendimento, porque não passariam de movimentos externos da substancia
interior, destinados a não influir sobre o homem, senão como simples
quebra da monotonia universal, claro estava que a sua existencia não
impediria a felicidade humana.
o_humanitismo.translation: The social reforms of the method of the dellies did not eliminate war, insurrection, simple beatings, anonymous stabbing, poverty, hunger, disease; but since these supposed scourges were true misunderstandings, because they were nothing more than external movements of the inner substance, intended not to influence man, but merely to break the monotony of the universe, it was clear that their existence would not prevent human happiness.

o_humanitismo.original:

de antigos tempos, nem por isso ficava destruido o systema, e por dous
motivos: 1.° porque sendo Humanitas a substancia creadora e absoluta,
cada individuo deveria achar a maior delicia do mundo em sacrificar-se
ao principio de que descende; 2.° porque, ainda assim, não diminuiria o
poder espiritual do homem sobre a terra, inventada unicamente para seu
recreio delle, como as estrellas, as brisas, as tamaras e o rhuibarbo.
o_humanitismo.translation: But even when these scourges (which was radically false) corresponded in the future to the convoluted conception of ancient times, the system was not destroyed for this reason, and for two reasons: 1. because Humanitas being the creative and absolute substance, each individual should find the greatest delight in the world in sacrificing himself to the principle from which he descends; 2. because it would still not diminish man's spiritual power on earth, invented solely for his recreation of the stars, the breezes, the tamaras and the rhubarb.

theoria_do_beneficio.translation: I am not saying that even without this circumstance, sometimes the memory of the gift does not persist, accompanied by a certain more or less intense affection; but these are true aberrations, of no value in the eyes of a philosopher.

theoria_do_beneficio.original:
Primeiramente, ha o sentimento de uma boa acção, e

theoria_do_beneficio.translation: First, there is the feeling of good action, and deductively the awareness that we are capable of good actions; secondly, there is a conviction of superiority over another creature, superiority in state and means; and this is one of the most legitimately pleasing things, according to the best opinions, to the human organism.

theoria_do_beneficio.original:
Estou longe de rejeitar essa observação de

theoria_do_beneficio.translation: I am far from rejecting Erasmus's observation, but I will say what she did not say, namely, that if one of the asses scratches the other better, he must have in his eyes some special indication of satisfaction.

a_semi_demencia.translation: The voice could hardly get out of my chest; and I had not discovered the whole cruel truth.

a_semi_demencia.original:
O Quincas Borba não só estava louco, mas sabia que
estava louco, e esse resto de consciencia, como uma frouxa lamparina
no meio das trevas, complicava muito o horror da situação.
a_semi_demencia.translation: The Fifth Fool was not only mad, but he knew he was mad, and that rest of consciousness, like a soft flashlight in the middle of the dark, greatly complicated the horror of the situation.

21: Other, of a kind used for the manufacture of goods

a_semi_demencia.original:
Sabia-o,
e não se irritava contra o mal; ao contrario, dizia-me que era ainda
uma prova de Humanitas, que assim brincava comsigo mesmo.
a_semi_demencia.translation: I knew it, and he wasn't angry at evil; on the contrary, he told me it was still a test of Humanitas, which was really playing with me.

a_semi_demencia.original:
Recitava-me
longos capitulos do livro, e antiphonas, e litanias espirituaes; chegou
até a reproduzir uma dansa sacra que inventara para as ceremonias do
Humanitismo.
a_semi_demencia.translation: He recited to me long chapters of the book, and antiphons, and spiritual litanies; he even played a sacred dance he had invented for the ceremonies of Humanism.

7: Other, of a kind used for the manufacture of goods

a_semi_demencia.original:
A graça lugubre com que elle levantava e sacudia as pernas
era singularmente fantastica.
a_semi_demencia.translation: The gloomy grace with which she raised and shook her legs was singularly fantastic.

9: the following table shows the following:

a_semi_demencia.original:
Outras vezes amuava-se a um canto, com os
olhos fitos no ar, uns olhos em que, de longe em longe, fulgurava um
raio persistente da razão, triste como uma lagrima... Morreu pouco tempo depois, em minha casa, jurando e repetindo sempre
que a dor era uma illusão, e que Pangloss, o calumniado Pangloss, não
era tão tolo como o suppoz Voltaire.
a_semi_demencia.translation: Other times he would be mucking around a corner, with his eyes fixed in the air, eyes in which, from afar to far away, a persistent ray of reason would shine, sad as a tear... he died shortly afterwards in my house, swearing and repeating every time that pain was an illusion, and that Pangloss, the slandered Pangloss, was not as foolish as I suppose Voltaire was.

3: Other, of a kind used for the manufacture of goods

das_negativas.original:
Entre a morte do Quincas Borba e a minha, mediaram os successos
narrados na primeira parte do livro.
das_negativas.translation: Between the death of the Fifth Fowl and mine, the successes recounted in the first part of the book mediated.

das_negativas.original:
O principal delles foi a invenção
doemplasto Braz Cubas, que morreu commigo, por causa da molestia
que apanhei.
das_negativas.translation: The main reason for this was the invention of the Braz Cubas implant, which died like me because of the trouble I got.

9: Other, of a thickness of not more than 10 mm
16: The following is the list of the following:

das_negativas.original:
Divino emplasto, tu me darias o primeiro logar entre os
homens, acima da sciencia e da riqueza, porque eras a genuina e directa
inspiração do ceu.
das_negativas.translation: Divine placement, you would give me the first log among men, above science and wealth, because you were the genuine and direct inspiration of heaven.

2: Other, of a kind used for the manufacture of goods

das_negativas.original:
O acaso determinou o contrario; e ahi vos ficaes
eternamente hypocondriacos.
das_negativas.translation: The chance has determined otherwise, and you will remain there forever hypocrites.

7: ♪ I 'm going to be a little late ♪
11: Other, of a kind used for the manufacture of goods

das_negativas.original: Este ultimo capitulo é todo de negativas.
das_negativas.translation: This last chapter is all about negatives.

das_negativas.original:
Não alcancei a celebridade do
emplasto, não fui ministro, não fui califa, não conheci o casamento.
das_negativas.translation: I didn't get the celebrity of the plot, I wasn't a minister, I wasn't a caliph, I didn't know about marriage.

6: Other, of a kind used for the manufacture of goods

das_negativas.original:
Verdade é que, ao lado dessas faltas, coube-me a boa fortuna de não
comprar o pão com o suor do meu rosto.
das_negativas.translation: The truth is, alongside these flaws, I am fortunate not to buy bread with the sweat of my face.

das_negativas.original:
Mais: não padeci a morte de D.
Placida, nem a semi-demencia do Quincas Borba.
das_negativas.translation: Plus, I didn't suffer the death of D. Placida, or the dementia of the Fifth Fool.

11: Other, not elsewhere specified or included

das_negativas.original:
Sommadas umas cousas e
outras, qualquer pessoa imaginará que não houve mingua nem sobra, e
conseguintemente que sai quite com a vida.
das_negativas.translation: Putting together a few things and other things, anyone would imagine that there was no mingua or excess, and consequently that he would go on living.

das_negativas.original:
E imaginará mal; porque ao
chegar a este outro lado do mysterio, achei-me com um pequeno saldo,
que é a derradeira negativa deste capitulo de negativas:—Não tive
filhos, não transmitti a nenhuma creatura o legado da nossa miseria.
das_negativas.translation: And you'll think it's wrong, because when I got to this other side of the mystery, I found myself with a small balance, which is the last negative of this chapter of negatives: I didn't have children, I didn't pass on to any creature the legacy of our misery.
